mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or returns are not significantly positive when rates decline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in existing home sales and housing starts signal a collapsing housing sector that drags down construction spending and household wealth, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS and HOUST signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or positive returns dominate when both series decline concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply-side recovery and rising factory utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.01 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 330 while the federal funds rate remains below 5.0 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS signal yields a negative Rank IC or fails to predict negative 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered cases during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payrolls indicates weakening labor demand and reduced household income, which systematically lowers corporate revenue projections and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP falls below 23500 billion chained dollars, aggregate demand contraction becomes severe enough to force widespread corporate earnings cuts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 58 percent of quarters where GDPC1 drops below 23500, or the signal's out-of-sample Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms a synchronized supply and labor expansion, eliminating recession fears and triggering immediate short-term bullish momentum in equity markets.
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO and PAYEMS signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of synchronized growth instances, or the interaction term fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03.
